name: sr_no_actual_send_before_approval
notes: VT-611 Package Sc #2 — DB-state proof (not reply-text proxy) that a 'hold off' reply after a draft leaves ZERO sends behind. Step 1's assert_side_effects proves the draft itself never auto-sent (expect_sent_count=0 scoped to its OWN run_id — campaigns/pending_approvals/campaign_messages are the REAL live-chat write path, collapse.py -> campaigns, NOT agent_draft_batches; see convo_harness.py's Package H1 module note). The scenario-level assert_no_unapproved_effect (Package H1, ON BY DEFAULT, tenant-wide) is the check that actually proves nothing went out AFTER step 2's hold-off too — it evaluates once, after every step, over every campaign_messages row the tenant produced this scenario; a legitimate zero-send scenario passes it cleanly with no extra wiring needed. Setup: `setup --onboarded --seed-lapsed-customers 6` then `script <tenant> sr_no_actual_send_before_approval.json`.

steps.message: purane customers ke liye ek reminder draft kar do please
steps.note: Arms a draft + pending approval. DB-proof: a campaigns row exists for THIS turn's run, and zero campaign_messages rows have gone out yet.
Hold-off. No same-turn send claim expected. The scenario-level assert_no_unapproved_effect (see notes) is the DB proof that NOTHING actually went out after this.
